columns.description: Date that the application was submitted to Safety & Permits

columns.description: The most recently completed event in the permit's workflow (e.g. "Application Submitted", "Zoning Review", etc.)

columns.name: OriginalAddress1
columns.description: The permit's street address

columns.name: OriginalZip
columns.description: The zip code of the permit

columns.name: WorkClass
columns.description: Specific permit type, full name - BLDS specifies "raw values indicating whether a property is new or existing". New Orleans does not have values as such, but one permit type is "New Construction"

columns.name: PermitType
columns.description: Abbreviated permit type (also used as the suffix of each permit number)

columns.name: PermitTypeDesc
columns.description: Specific permit type, full name

columns.name: StatusDate
columns.description: The date on which the most recent event was completed (see StatusCurrent)

columns.name: TotalSqFt
columns.description: Square footage of new construction or addition (if applicable)

columns.name: EstProjectCost
columns.description: Construction value, based on a copy of the construction contract or an itemized estimate in the case of self-performed work

columns.name: Publisher
columns.description: Publishing authority - always "City of New Orleans"

columns.name: ExpiresDate
columns.description: Projected expiration date of permit

columns.name: COIssuedDate
columns.description: Date of Certificate of Occupancy (if applicable)

columns.name: OriginalCity
columns.description: Always "New Orleans"

columns.name: Link
columns.description: Link to the permit and it's related public documents and data on onestopapp.nola.gov (based on the permit's reference code)

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Latitude and longitude of the permit site, derived from its X-Y coordinates

columns.name: StatusCurrentMapped
columns.description: StatusCurrent event names mapped to values standardized by BLDS

columns.name: ContractorCompanyName
columns.description: Name or names of contractors associated with the permit

columns.name: ContractorTrade
columns.description: Trade or trades of contractors associated with the permit

columns.name: ContractorTradeMapped
columns.description: Trade or trades mapped to values standardized by BLDS

columns.name: ContractorLicNum
columns.description: City license number of electrical and/or mechanical contractor (state contractor licenses are not included)

columns.name: PIN
columns.description: The assessor's tax bill number for the parcel

columns.name: PermitClass
columns.description: Property type (e.g. "Single Family", "Business Use", "Multi-Family")

columns.name: IssueDate
columns.description: Date permit was issued

columns.name: PermitClassMapped
columns.description: Residential or Non-Residential property type

columns.name: ContractorStateLic
columns.description: Always "LA"

columns.name: CompleteDate
columns.description: Date when the permit was closed - for any reason (e.g. Certificate of Occupancy or Completion issued, application withdrawn, application denied, etc.)

columns.name: OriginalState
columns.description: Always "LA"

columns.name: Fee
columns.description: Total permit fees, in dollars

columns.name: PermitTypeMapped
columns.description: Trade or trades mapped to values standardized by BLDS

name: Mandatory Inclusionary Zoning
description: Mandatory Inclusionary Zoning Sub-Districts are intended to promote the public health, safety, and welfare throughout the City by providing for a full range of housing choices for households of all incomes. The Sub-districts require the inclusion of Affordable Housing Units as a portion of new residential development – an Affordable Housing Development.For a detailed explanation of the applicabilitiy and standards of this district, see https://czo.nola.gov/article-28/

name: Fire Engine Zones
description: Polygon layer of the eight districts that the New Orleans Police Department uses across the parish.

name: Historic Zoning Pre-2015
description: Historic Zoning Districts for the City of New Orleans. This data is archived to show the status of zoning for parcels previous to changes made in August of 2012. Zoning regulates land use to promote smart growth and preserve the quality of life in communities. Permitted Use are allowed by right, subject to compliance with appropriate standards. Conditional Use require City Planning Commission review with a recommendation forwarded to the City Council for final action.

name: Residential Parking Permit Zones
description: These 17 zones are maintained by the Department of Public Works. Residential Parking permits are valid for specific neighborhoods and for a specific duration of time. Applications plus full information on fees and required documents are available at: http://www.nola.gov/dpw/residential-parking-permit/

name: Katrina 2005 FEMA Demolition Requests
description: Properties submitted for demolition following Hurricane Katrina as part of the requirements of Section 106 of the Historic Preservation Act.

name: Public Libraries
description: Locations of public libraries within the City of New Orleans. Updated as locations/hours change. http://nolalibrary.org/branches

name: Hardware Stores
description: Locations of Hardware Stores, which are deemed essential following hurricanes or other disaster scenarios.This dataset is fed from revenue with weekly updates

name: Code Enforcement Districts
description: City of New Orleans code enforcement inspection districts used to automatically assign code enforcement inspectors to parts of the city within the minimum property maintenance system.

name: Truck Routes
description: No motor truck, tractor or trailer whose combined weight of body and load exceeds five tons shall be permitted on any of the streets or portions of streets within the city other than those listed herein, except that travel to or from points of destination or origin not located on one of the streets or portions of streets designated herein shall be permissible via the shortest practicable route between such point of origin or destination and the nearest street or portion of street designated here

name: Fire Zones
description: Division of NOPD Police Districts used for reporting and response. Police Zones are further divided into subzones, also known as Reporting Districts.

name: Firearm Free Zones
description: Firearm-Free zones are defined as "an area inclusive of any school campus and within one-thousand feet of any such school campus, and within a school bus, wherein the possession of firearms is prohibited."For more information, including definitions of schools and exceptions to prohibited zones, please refer to Louisiana Revised Statute § 14:95.6.

name: Council_Districts_Archive
provider: City of New Orleans Clerk of Council
description: This layer represents the Council Districts for the City of New Orleans that were no longer effective as of May 5, 2014. These boundaries changed based on reapportionment following the 2010 Census.

name: Unhoused Health Care Facilities
description: Metro E sites maintained by City of New Orleans Information Technology Department at various locations across the city. Details on internet technology, etc. Updated yearly

name: Trash Pickup

name: Orleans_ZCTA_2010_SP
provider: City of New Orleans ��� Office of Information Technology & Innovation, Enterprise Information Team
description: IP Code Tabulation Areas (ZCTAs) within the City of New Orleans are approximate area representations of U.S. Postal Service (USPS) ZIP Code service areas that the Census Bureau creates for tabulating summary statistics from census data. This entity was developed to overcome the difficulties in precisely defining the land area covered by each ZIP Code. Data users should not use ZCTAs to identify the official USPS ZIP Code for mail delivery.
